food_subgroup: Fried dough pastry assortment with dipping sauces

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general churro entry (FOODON_03302114). The Funbox is a branded assortment of classic and krispy churros with multiple dipping sauces; closest_parent is appropriate as no exact multi-piece assortment code exists.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers fried dough products broadly. No specific churros assortment code exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for churros or fried dough dessert assortments.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Funbox contains 10 classic churros, 10 krispy churros, and 3 dipping sauces/toppings, estimated at approximately 500 g total for the full shareable box.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, fried dough pastry — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for churros
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'The Funbox' assortment exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros (fried dough, wheat-based, sugared) and adjusted to reflect the mixed classic/krispy format with dipping sauces. The dipping sauces (chocolate, caramel, cream cheese) contribute additional sugar, fat, and energy; values represent a weighted composite estimate for the full assortment per 100 g edible portion. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried dough / doughnuts and similar wheat-flour fried pastries — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated by analogy with fried wheat-flour dough products (doughnuts, GI ~76 per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Churros share a similar composition: refined wheat flour, deep-fried, with added sugar. The presence of dipping sauces (chocolate, caramel) further elevates the effective glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 255) / 100 ≈ 194, based on estimated 500 g serving with 51 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) and high energy density from deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of this fried dough assortment, as the sodium content from the dough and dipping sauces can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg; sharing the box across multiple people is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~76), high refined carbohydrate load (~51 g/100 g), and added sugars from dipping sauces
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this churros assortment as an occasional indulgence only, as the high GI and substantial glycemic load (GL ~194 per full box) will cause a rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with protein or fibre-rich foods and strict portion control are essential if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (18.5 g/100 g), saturated fat (4.8 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and high energy density from deep-frying in oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this deep-fried churros assortment due to its elevated saturated and trans fat content, which can adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; the high caloric density also poses a risk for weight gain, a key CVD risk factor.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Funbox is a shareable churros assortment offered by Churros Republic at their Funan outlet in Singapore, available via field survey. It contains 10 pieces of classic churros, 10 pieces of krispy churros, and 3 dipping sauces and toppings. Churros are a traditional Spanish fried dough pastry made from a choux-like wheat flour dough piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crispy. Classic churros are the traditional straight or looped variety dusted with cinnamon sugar, while krispy churros feature a lighter, crispier texture. The dipping sauces typically include chocolate, caramel, and a cream cheese or similar dairy-based dip. The texture is crispy on the outside and soft or slightly chewy on the inside, with a rich, sweet, and lightly savoury flavour profile. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense (~390 kcal/100 g), high in refined carbohydrates (~51 g/100 g), and moderately high in fat (~18.5 g/100 g) due to deep-frying. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 76 (high), making this a high-glycemic food with a very high glycemic load per full serving. Clinically, this food is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and Cardiovascular Disease due to its high GI, refined carbohydrate content, saturated fat, and trans fat. It is also of moderate concern for those managing hypertension due to sodium from the dough and sauces. The Funbox is designed as a party or sharing dessert and is best consumed occasionally and in small portions.
common_user_queries: What is The Funbox from Churros Republic?
How many churros are in The Funbox?
Calories in churros assortment box Singapore
What sauces come with The Funbox?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'The Funbox' churros assortment exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain fried churros and adjusted for the mixed classic/krispy format with dipping sauces. GI is estimated by analogy with fried wheat-flour dough products from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite and analogous estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for fried wheat-flour dough (churros/doughnuts) and adjusted for the mixed assortment format including dipping sauces. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific product would be required for high confidence. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central churros reference entry. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source for this food type. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as churros are a plant-based fried dough product and B12 is not reported in the reference source. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source for this food type. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source for this food type. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source for this food type. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference source for this food type. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; fried dough pastries contain no vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central reference data. 10. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on fried wheat-flour dough products (doughnuts) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) via the University of Sydney GI Database. 11. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant's field survey listing; independent halal certification should be verified. 12. The Funbox is a branded menu item from Churros Republic — Funan, Singapore; nutrient values represent a per-100 g composite estimate for the full assortment including sauces.
